reasoning: The FRED trend analysis provides a primary combined estimate of 0.71 for an increase above 5.25. This high probability primarily stems from the linear model projecting a bounce back to recent averages after a sharp drop in yields during early February (from 5.43 on Feb 4 to 5.25 on Feb 13). Because AAA corporate yields and 10-year Treasury yields fundamentally behave similarly to random walks rather than mean-reverting linearly, the 71% statistical anchor is overly confident. The drop to multi-month lows indicates real macroeconomic momentum on changing Federal Reserve rate cut expectations, rather than easily reversible noise. I balance the provided 71% statistical anchor with the random-walk nature of yields, adjusting my near-term (Feb 22) probability to 58%, anticipating a slight chance for a minor technical bounce. For the more distant resolution date (Mar 17), increasing uncertainty over the 1-month horizon draws the probability downwards, closer to the 50% random-walk baseline, resulting in 54%.
